 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","title":"Wills & Estate Planning skill launch article","description":"# Wills & Estate Planning skill — launch article + gated install UX

## What this epic covers

Ship a full-length launch article at `/writing/wills-and-estate-planning` that does two things at once:

1. **Promotes** the new `wills-and-estate-planning-skill` on jeffreys-skills.md (the biggest skill yet — 201 files, 572-line SKILL.md, 17 subagents, 45 deliverable templates, 135 reference docs, 8 operating modes, 5 wealth tiers, 11 axioms, 12 cognitive operators, 9 intake phases). Target audience is explicitly **non-technical** — office workers, parents, entrepreneurs, retirees — not just developers.
2. **Explains** the simplest-possible non-terminal install flow: paste a signed agent prompt into Claude Desktop or Codex Desktop, and the agent does the rest. Leverages the paid-jsm.md session in the users browser to gate bundle downloads via short-lived single-use codes. Mirrors the existing `jsm login` auth pattern.

## Why this matters

The tax-preparation skill generated real user demand and real savings ($1k+ per user, with one user saving $20k). An X post teasing the wills skill got strong engagement. But these skills can only become mainstream if *non-developers* can install them. Todays jsm.md install UX requires `jsm install <slug>` in a terminal, which excludes 90% of the target market.

The article serves a secondary purpose: its a **case study in agent-friendly UX**. The paste-a-prompt-into-an-agent pattern generalizes to any paid skill on jsm.md, not just wills, so the article doubles as a template for future non-technical launches.

## Architectural pillars

- **No public GitHub repo** for skill content. Everything gated behind jsm.md auth.
- **Short-lived single-use install codes** minted server-side, bound to `(user_id, skill_id, exp, consumed_at)`.
- **Agent-driven install**: the gated bundle URL is embedded in a copy-paste prompt that the users Claude/Codex agent executes. One tool-use approval on the desktop, no terminal keystrokes from the user.
- **Works on every OS** (macOS / Windows / Linux) because the agent handles path/unzip — no native helper, no code signing required.
- **Bundle watermark** inside the ZIPs README with user_id + timestamp. Deterrent against casual leaks; does not prevent determined piracy (acknowledged trade-off).

## Cross-repo scope

- **This repo (`jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site`)**: the article + hero illustration + 5 custom visualizations + the agent-readable primer markdown in `/public/` + OG images + writing-index registration + command-palette entry + Playwright test.
- **`/dp/jeffreys-skills.md`** (separate bead graph): DB migration + two endpoints + install-card UI + generic non-technical-user guide page. Tracked in that repos .beads/.

## Acceptance criteria

- Article live at `/writing/wills-and-estate-planning` with hero, TOC, 8+ long-form sections, 5 interactive visualizations, OG + Twitter images, and the tri-tab install section.
- Primer markdown downloadable from the article.
- Article visible in the writing index + command palette.
- `bun run build` green, `bun tsc --noEmit` green, `bun lint` green, `ubs` green on changed files.
- Playwright e2e smoke test covers: article renders, TOC links jump, visualizations mount, install-tab switches.
- Depends on jsm.md beads being shipped for the install prompt URLs to resolve; acceptable to ship article first with a "coming soon" placeholder in the install section if jsm.md side is not ready.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:33:55.236585249Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T22:33:55.236585249Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.1","title":"Scaffold article route + metadata (app/writing/wills-and-estate-planning/page.tsx)","description":"Create `app/writing/wills-and-estate-planning/page.tsx` modeled on `app/writing/slack-mattermost-migration/page.tsx`:

- `export const metadata` with title "Drafting a Serious Estate Plan on a Saturday | Jeffrey Emanuel" (or final chosen title), description, and `alternates.canonical` = `/writing/wills-and-estate-planning`.
- `articleSchema` JSON-LD (@type Article) with headline, datePublished (2026-04-XX on launch), author, publisher, mainEntityOfPage, `about` array including: Estate Planning, Wills, Trusts, AI Agents, Agent Skills, Claude Code, Codex, Probate, Incapacity Planning.
- Default export renders `<JsonLd data={articleSchema} />` and `<WillsEstateArticle />`.

## Why this bead exists
Next.js App Router needs the route stub in place before the component file can be referenced. Keeping it tiny + isolated lets us ship the article component independently.

## Acceptance criteria
- File exists with metadata + JsonLd + default export.
- `bun tsc --noEmit` clean (will error until the article component is also scaffolded; that is OK — this bead blocks on the component scaffold bead).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:34:23.442042493Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T22:43:33.701359737Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.1","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:34:23.442042493Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.1","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:43:33.700736019Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.10","title":"Skill catalog callouts (jsm.md links + what shipped in latest release)","description":"Add the paired-callout block directly under the intro, mirroring the slack articles catalog/release pattern.

## Callout 1: Skill catalog page
Single prominent link card:

- **Eyebrow**: `Skill catalog page · opens in a new tab`
- **Link**: `https://jeffreys-skills.md/skills/wills-and-estate-planning-skill` (dotted-underline, cyan)
- **Caption**: "Full SKILL.md, live visualization, version history, release notes, and the Install in Claude or Codex Desktop button."
- **Heads up**: "That URL returns a 404 unless youre signed in to a jeffreys-skills.md account with an active subscription. Sign up (or log in) at jeffreys-skills.md/dashboard first. Thats the same account the install flow below uses, so doing it now means the install prompt will already know who you are."

## Callout 2: What shipped in the latest release
Match slacks "What shipped in the latest release" pattern:

- **Eyebrow**: `What shipped in the latest release · April 2026`
- Sections:
  - `v1` **the kernel**: 12 axioms, 14 cognitive operators (Probate-Bypass, Spousal-Rights Check, Beneficiary-Title Coherence, Step-Up-vs-Transfer, Liquidity-at-Death, Incapacity-Transition, Lumpy-Asset Division, Cross-State Domicile, Vulnerable-Beneficiary Filter, Tax-Apportionment, Blended-Family QTIP, Disclaimer Window, Trust-Situs Selection, Basis-Consistency), 5 wealth tiers with complexity overlays.
  - `v1` **the intake engine**: 9 adaptive phases (Orientation → Wealth-Tier Routing), 8 operating modes (new-plan / existing-plan-audit / life-event-delta / urgent-bedside-signing / executor-activation / business-owner-succession / uhnw-restructure / maintenance-review), archetype start packs.
  - `v1` **the output contract**: 2 intake files, 20 analyses, 23 deliverables — 45 structured artifacts in total. The contract is enforced: `plan-validator.py` runs as a backstop.
  - `v1` **17 subagents** (intake-conductor, document-organizer, asset-discovery-auditor, beneficiary-audit, anti-pattern-scanner, tax-analyzer, execution-formalities-router, state-law-verifier, overlay-resolver, fiduciary-bench-builder, implementation-ops-planner, funding-checklist-generator, conflict-prevention-planner, litigation-defense-reviewer, multi-model-validator, deliverables-generator, and a meta coordinator).
  - `v1` **the reference corpus**: 135 markdown docs across methodology, intake, foundations, family-structures, assets, incapacity, advanced-planning, tiers, post-death, legacy-and-logistics, anti-patterns, states, professions, life-events, situations, execution-formalities.
  - `v1` **verification-first discipline**: every recommendation that depends on volatile law (2026 OBBBA $15M exemption, state estate tax thresholds, execution formalities) must be checked against primary sources and logged in `analyses/official-source-log.md`.

## Acceptance criteria
- Looks substantially like the slack articles equivalent block.
- All skill-page URLs point at jeffreys-skills.md.
- Heads-up paragraph about 404-unless-authenticated is present — this prevents confused readers from thinking the site is broken.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:37:03.276128406Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:12.596716476Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.10","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:37:03.276128406Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.10","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:12.596037324Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}
-{"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.11","title":"Install section with three tabs (agent paste / jsm CLI / manual ZIP)","description":"The single most important section of the article: the non-technical install flow. This is the payoff of the entire auth-code design.

## Section structure (id="install")
SectionHeader eyebrow="Install" title="Get the skill into your agent in 90 seconds."

Then three labeled tabs/cards (Id lean toward 3 stacked cards on mobile and a tab group on desktop — build it as 3 cards with subtle emphasis on card #1):

### Card 1: "In Claude or Codex Desktop (recommended, no terminal)" — default/emphasized

1. **Short pitch**: If youre not a developer, this is the only install path you need. You already have Claude Desktop or Codex Desktop installed (if not, link to the download pages). You have a paid jeffreys-skills.md subscription. Thats it.

2. **Step 1**: Visit the skill page on jeffreys-skills.md (link). Make sure youre signed in.

3. **Step 2**: Click `Generate install prompt`. The site mints a one-time, 10-minute-TTL, single-use code tied to your account and gives you a small block of text to copy.

4. **Step 3**: Open Claude or Codex Desktop. Paste the text. The agent reads it, asks you once to approve the single download tool call (standard "allow this command?" dialog), downloads the bundle, extracts it to the right folder, and tells you to reload skills.

5. **Step 4**: The skill appears in the skill picker. You type `/wills` or just describe what you want (`"I want to review my existing will — I moved from CA to TX last year"`) and the agent routes through the skill.

**Callout**: `Why this works without a terminal` — "The agent IS the terminal. You never type a command; you approve one. The URL embedded in the prompt is a single-use token bound to your paid session, so even if you paste the prompt into someone elses computer it wont work there: the code expires before it can be reused."

### Card 2: "Via the jsm CLI (for developers)"
- Same as slack articles pattern: install jsm, login, `jsm install wills-and-estate-planning-skill`.
- Also mention `claude plugins install` alternative if/when that channel is supported.
- Keep tight — this is the familiar path.

### Card 3: "Manual download (fallback)"
- When the agent-paste flow doesnt work (e.g. the user is running Claude in Chrome or a constrained environment), they can click `Download bundle (.zip)` on the skill page. Same single-use code under the hood.
- Instructions: unzip, then drag `wills-and-estate-planning-skill/` into `~/.claude/skills/` (macOS/Linux) or `%USERPROFILE%\.claude\skills\` (Windows). Same for `~/.codex/skills/` or `~/.agents/skills/` if using Codex.
- Brief mention that theres a "reveal folder" helper button on the skill page that opens Finder/Explorer pointing at the right location.

## Pedagogical aside
After card 3, a short explanatory paragraph about the auth-code flow: the signed URL is how jeffreys-skills.md proves to itself that the paid subscriber clicked the button. Its the same pattern as `jsm login` that developers already know, just re-aimed at install.

## Dependency / coordination
This sections copy should mention specific URLs like `jeffreys-skills.md/skills/wills-and-estate-planning-skill`. The jsm.md-side beads need to have the install-card UI live before this sections instructions are technically accurate. Acceptable to ship the article with the UI partially live and update in a follow-up PR.

## Acceptance criteria
- Three cards render correctly on mobile and desktop.
- Each card has a clear CTA pointing at jsm.md.
- No mock code blocks that imply the user must type bash — the agent does everything.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:37:30.447308428Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:12.715172110Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.11","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:37:30.447308428Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.11","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:12.714423898Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.12","title":"'Who this is for' section with tier-triage visualization","description":"Address the question every reader has after the install section: "does this apply to me?"

## Section structure (id="who")
SectionHeader eyebrow="Scope" title="Who this is for (and who it isnt)."

### Opening paragraph
Most people think estate planning is for rich people. It is not. Its for anyone who owns anything, cares about anyone, or would prefer a county court not decide what happens to their kids. The skill scales from Tier 1 (renter, mid-20s, no dependents) through Tier 5 ($100M+ industrialist with a private foundation), and the same interview adapts itself to whichever tier matches your facts.

### Embed the TierTriageViz visualization
(Visualization implemented in a separate bead — `wills-estate-visualizations.tsx`.)

Interactive: five rungs of a wealth ladder, each with:
- Net worth band
- Archetype ("young-family renter", "suburban homeowner + 401(k)", "founder with RSUs + SLAT candidate", "industrialist / dynasty")
- Primary goals (guardianship, probate avoidance, transfer tax minimization, family governance)
- Core deliverables (will, POAs, beneficiary audit → revocable trust + QTIP → GRAT + IDGT + dynasty trust, etc.)

Tapping a rung expands an inline detail panel. Default selection should be Tier 2 (middle-class homeowner), since thats the modal reader.

### Complexity overlays
Below the viz, a short list of "what bumps you up a tier regardless of net worth":
- Blended family (+1)
- Non-citizen spouse (+1, QDOT territory)
- Privately held business (+1)
- Disabled or vulnerable heir requiring SNT (+1)
- Multi-state or multi-country assets (+1)
- Specialty items: firearms (NFA trust), crypto self-custody, creator-economy IP, pre-IPO stock, addiction/capacity concerns.

### Who this is NOT for (honest scope limits)
- Non-U.S. domiciliaries. The skill is U.S. federal + state + local; foreign-asset overlays exist but the core is U.S. law.
- People who need a will *today* (bedside signing). The skill has an `urgent-bedside-signing` mode, but for true emergencies you want a lawyer on the phone, not an agent.
- People who want a single-page "just write my will" interaction. The skill is deliberately thorough — it produces 45 artifacts — because coherence across documents is the whole point. If you just want a $30 LegalZoom will, go buy one.

## Acceptance criteria
- Visualization renders and is keyboard-accessible.
- Scope limits are explicit — reduces complaints post-launch.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:37:52.573101216Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:14.385022588Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.12","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:37:52.573101216Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.12","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.21","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:14.384369815Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.12","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:12.838960023Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.13","title":"Core insight section: Axiom 0 + Axiom 1, one coherent story","description":"The thesis section. This is the part that, if the reader leaves with nothing else, should still change how they think about estate planning.

## Section structure (id="insight")
SectionHeader eyebrow="The thesis" title="Why a will alone is not the plan."

### Narrative beats

1. **The most common failure mode is not a drafting error; it is incoherence across documents.** A beautifully-drafted will that says "everything to my kids" does nothing if the 401(k) still names an ex-spouse, because retirement accounts pass by *contract*, not by will. Axiom 0: the will does not control the whole estate.

2. **The plan is the whole story.** Will + revocable trust + beneficiary forms + deeds + POAs + healthcare directives + letter of instruction + digital inventory must all point at the same people, in the same shares, under the same contingencies, under the same states law. Axiom 1: one coherent story.

3. **Concrete example (drive it home)**: "Imagine you die tonight. Your spouse has to locate and call eight institutions in seven days: your 401(k) administrator, two IRA custodians, your life-insurance company, your countys register of deeds, your bank, your brokerage, and the guardian you named for your kids. Each of them will ask a different question. Each of them will accept a different document as proof. The skills job is to make sure the answer they get, and the document you produced, match — so your spouse doesnt spend six months learning probate law at the worst possible time."

### Embed the AxiomCoherenceViz visualization
An interactive SVG/D3 diagram showing:
- Center node: "You"
- 8 spokes out to asset/decision categories (house, 401k, IRA, life insurance, brokerage, POAs, healthcare, digital).
- Each spoke shows which document controls that asset (will / trust / beneficiary form / titling / POA / etc.).
- Toggle "incoherence mode" that highlights mismatches — e.g., a will saying "to kids" but the 401(k) still naming the ex-spouse turns that spoke red.
- Interactive: click a spoke, read a sentence-long plain-English explanation.

### Reinforce with 3 more axioms
- **Axiom 2**: Plan for incapacity first, death second. Dementia, stroke, and coma damage more wealth than death, and lasts longer.
- **Axiom 5**: Under the $15M exemption (2026 OBBBA), basis often dominates tax. Most families are better off *not* gifting appreciated assets out of the estate.
- **Axiom 10**: Communication is the actual work. Surprise at the reading of the will is one of the fastest ways to turn a plan into litigation.

(The other 7 axioms live in the primer and SKILL.md — dont try to cover all 11 here; the article is not a replacement for the skill.)

## Tone discipline
- Every technical term should have a `<J>` tooltip.
- Use names and stakes. Not "elective share." "The rule that says your spouse can override your will and take roughly a third no matter what your will says."
- No code.

## Acceptance criteria
- Visualization mounts, works on touch devices, respects reduced-motion.
- At least one reader test (a non-technical friend/family member) should read this section and be able to articulate "the will is not the plan" back to you.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:38:16.997426357Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:14.524588227Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.13","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:38:16.997426357Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.13","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.22","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:14.523855615Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.13","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:12.961771157Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.14","title":"Sample session walkthrough (blended-family HNW archetype with IntakePhasesViz)","description":"The emotional centerpiece. A narrated sample session that makes the 9 intake phases concrete.

## Archetype chosen
A **Tier 3 HNW blended family** — because it hits the most instructive patterns in one session:
- Second marriage (both spouses have first-marriage kids)
- One adult child from first marriage is in active addiction recovery
- Primary residence in NY (state estate tax!), vacation home in FL
- One spouse has a pre-IPO startup stake (concentrated position + liquidity risk)
- Family parrot (longer life expectancy than many humans — ties back to the hero illustration and the X-post hook)
- Modest crypto self-custody
- Previous will from 2018 (pre-OBBBA, pre-Secure Act, pre-remarriage)

This archetype cleanly triggers: elective-share, QTIP, vulnerable-heir SNT or incentive trust, NY-FL domicile analysis, liquidity-at-death operator, digital-asset inventory, pet-trust.

## Section structure (id="intake")
SectionHeader eyebrow="A sample session" title="What it actually feels like."

### Presentation
- Narrated from the users perspective ("Maya, 54, remarrying for the second time").
- 9 phases laid out as scenes. For each phase, show:
  - The agents conversational prompt (plain English, not forms).
  - Mayas answer (excerpted / paraphrased).
  - The artifact the agent quietly produces (e.g., `intake-record.md` appended).
  - Any landmine the agent flags as it goes (e.g., "Your ex-husband is still the primary beneficiary on the old 401(k). Flagging `red-flag-triage.md`.").
- After scene 5 (family dynamics), the agent triggers the `blended-family` overlay and the tone of the questions deepens.

### Embed IntakePhasesViz
Visualization showing all 9 phases as a horizontal flow (Orientation → People → Assets → Beneficiary Audit → Family Dynamics → Goals & Values → Incapacity → Jurisdiction → Tier Routing). Each node:
- Clickable — drills into what that phase asks
- Highlight when the narrative is "in" that phase
- Shows approximate time spent (5/10/15 min as in SKILL.md)

### Close the scene
- End with Maya getting a Comprehensive Plan Report, an Attorney Engagement Brief, and a 30-item Implementation Ledger — all produced across a weekend, rather than six months of attorney email threads.
- Crucial line: "The attorney she eventually hires doesnt look at this packet and say a robot did your planning. She looks at it and says good — youve done my diligence work. I can draft in three hours instead of fifteen, and Ill charge you for three."

## Why this archetype
The slack articles emotional centerpiece was the cutover moment. The wills article needs an equivalent — and the blended-family + addicted-child + parrot + crypto combo is vivid enough to stick. Avoid making the archetype so rich that no reader identifies with it; keep one or two elements (the remarriage, the kids) universal.

## Acceptance criteria
- Reads like narrative, not documentation.
- Every `<J>` tooltip works.
- IntakePhasesViz phase-linking animates smoothly as the reader scrolls through the scenes.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:38:42.043211696Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:14.657807067Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.14","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:38:42.043211696Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.14","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.23","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:14.657149425Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.14","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:13.086423399Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.29","title":"Lighthouse / Core Web Vitals check","description":"Run Lighthouse against the article on a dev build + production build.

## Targets
- **LCP** ≤ 2.5s (hero image must use `priority` + blur placeholder; already the pattern).
- **CLS** ≤ 0.1 (visualizations must reserve layout space before mount; use explicit width/height or `aspect-ratio`).
- **INP** ≤ 200ms (scroll-linked effects must be passive; visualizations must not block the main thread on mount).
- **Performance** ≥ 90.
- **Accessibility** = 100.
- **Best Practices** ≥ 95.
- **SEO** = 100.

## If targets fail
- Visualizations too heavy? Check bundle splits: each dynamic import should land in its own chunk. Consider further code-splitting (e.g., antipattern cards flip state machine).
- LCP late? Verify the hero image is in the initial HTML (not SSR-skipped), has `priority`, and uses a proper blur placeholder.
- CLS high? The usual suspect is a visualization whose height isnt reserved; add `min-h-[Npx]` to the viz wrapper.

## Acceptance criteria
- Lighthouse targets met on production build (Vercel preview deploy).
- Any failure is resolved at root cause before merge.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:43:00.532041834Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:16.798249640Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.29","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:43:00.532041834Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.29","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.28","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:16.797700271Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.3","title":"Build jargon glossary (lib/wills-estate-jargon.ts)","description":"Create `lib/wills-estate-jargon.ts` modeled exactly on `lib/slack-migration-jargon.ts`: export a `getJargon(term: string)` function that returns a `{ term, definition, ... }` record.

## Why
The article uses the `<J t="term">` component to produce dotted-underline tooltips over technical terms. Estate planning has a dense jargon layer (QTIP, GRAT, SLAT, ILIT, QDOT, DSUE, GST, QPRT, CRT, CLT, DAF, POD, TOD, RLT, POA, ERISA, Medicaid lookback, portability, step-up basis, elective share, community property, pretermitted heir, ancillary probate, Ulysses clause, etc.), and the article explicitly targets non-technical readers. Tooltips let us say "ILIT" in the text without derailing the reader.

## Term list (starter — expand as article sections go in)
- will, trust, revocable-trust, irrevocable-trust, pour-over-will, holographic-will
- POA, durable-POA, springing-POA, healthcare-proxy, living-will, HIPAA, POLST-MOLST
- beneficiary, contingent-beneficiary, per-stirpes, per-capita, portability, DSUE
- probate, intestacy, elective-share, pretermitted-heir, ancillary-probate, homestead
- QTIP, credit-shelter-trust, QDOT, ILIT, GRAT, SLAT, IDGT, QPRT, DAPT, dynasty-trust
- step-up-basis, disclaimer, §6166, 706-portability, GST-exemption
- titling, joint-tenancy, tenancy-by-entirety, community-property, TOD-deed, Lady-Bird-deed
- ERISA, 401k-spousal-consent, IRA-stretch, Secure-Act
- guardian, trustee, executor, fiduciary, co-trustee, successor-trustee
- domicile, snowbird, multi-state-probate
- asset-protection, Medicaid-lookback, SNT, ABLE, incentive-trust, Ulysses-clause
- NFA-gun-trust, crypto-seed, self-custody, legacy-contact
- charitable: DAF, CRT, CLT, private-foundation
- OBBBA, $15M-exemption, basis-dominance

## Structure per term
```ts
{
  term: "QTIP",
  full: "Qualified Terminable Interest Property trust",
  definition: "A trust for the surviving spouse where they get income for life, but the remainder goes to beneficiaries you chose (often first-marriage children). Prevents a widowed spouse from rewriting the plan and disinheriting the first marriage.",
  source: { label: "IRC §2056(b)(7)", href: "..." }  // optional
}
```

## Acceptance criteria
- File exists, ~40+ terms defined.
- Imported and used via `<J t="qtip">` inline in the article component.
- Each definition is plain-English (grandma-test: a reader who has never heard the term should understand it in one sentence).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:34:58.049538940Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T22:34:58.049538940Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.3","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:34:58.049538940Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.30","title":"Playwright smoke test for the wills-and-estate-planning article","description":"Add Playwright e2e test: `tests/wills-and-estate-planning.spec.ts` (or wherever existing tests live).

## Test scenarios
1. **Article renders**: visit `/writing/wills-and-estate-planning`, expect `h1` with "Drafting a Serious Estate Plan" (or final title) to be visible.
2. **TOC links work**: click each TOC link, expect the URL hash to change and the corresponding section heading to be in viewport.
3. **Install tabs switch**: on the install section, clicking each of the three tabs / cards reveals its content.
4. **Each visualization mounts** without console errors: scroll to each visualization, assert that the vizs root element has a known data-attribute (add `data-viz="tier-triage"` etc. in the viz beads for easy selection).
5. **Primer download button** produces a download request to `/wills-and-estate-planning-primer.md`.
6. **Mobile viewport** (`page.setViewportSize({ width: 375, height: 812 })`): article renders without horizontal scroll; no layout overflow.
7. **Reduced-motion**: set `page.emulateMedia({ reducedMotion: "reduce" })` and verify visualizations render statically (no animation-frame errors).

## Integration with existing test setup
Mirror `tests/slack-migration.spec.ts` (or whichever test file the slack article has). Reuse any shared helpers.

## Acceptance criteria
- `bunx playwright test tests/wills-and-estate-planning.spec.ts` passes in CI.

## Sequence
1. `bun run build` — zero errors.
2. `bun tsc --noEmit` — clean.
3. `bun lint` — clean.
4. `ubs <changed-files>` — exit 0.
5. `bunx playwright test` — existing suites + new wills-article smoke test all pass.
6. Visual QA on the Vercel preview deploy (one human pass through the article at desktop + mobile viewports).
7. `br sync --flush-only` then `git add .beads/ content/ components/ app/writing/wills-and-estate-planning/ lib/ public/ assets/ && git commit` with a message like: "feat(writing): launch wills-and-estate-planning article with 5 visualizations + gated agent-install flow".
8. Push to `main` (Vercel auto-deploys).
9. Verify production URL renders correctly.
10. Verify OG card renders when the URL is pasted into Twitter/X, Slack, LinkedIn.

## Acceptance criteria
- Article live at https://jeffreyemanuel.com/writing/wills-and-estate-planning.
- OG card renders correctly on at least Twitter/X and Slack.
- No production console errors.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:43:21.453768573Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:17.568637225Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.31","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:43:21.453768573Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.31","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.26","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:17.263462067Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.31","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.27","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:17.568096862Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.31","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.29","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:16.949387368Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.31","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.30","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:17.105540170Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.31","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:17.418534035Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}

+{"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.32","title":"Cross-repo coordination note: article install section ↔ jsm.md install UI","description":"# Cross-repo coordination note

## The dependency that crosses repo boundaries
- `jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.11` (article install section) references live URLs on jeffreys-skills.md: the skill detail page, the "Generate install prompt" button, etc.
- `bd-3t7zd.19` (jsm.md production deploy) is what makes those URLs real.

`br` does not natively track cross-repo dependencies. This bead exists so that whoever is working on the article remembers to either:
1. **Ship the article AFTER the jsm.md production deploy lands** — simplest, and the URLs in the install section will work on day one.
2. **Ship the article first with "coming soon" framing in the install section** — and file a follow-up bead to revise the article the day jsm.md goes live. Acceptable only if the jsm.md timeline slips.

## Coordination checklist (before pushing the article)
- [ ] `bd-3t7zd.19` (jsm.md prod deploy) is closed OR the articles install section is temporarily framed as "coming soon this week."
- [ ] The skill page URL in the article resolves: `curl -I https://jeffreys-skills.md/skills/wills-and-estate-planning-skill` returns 200 (or 302 → login; either is fine).
- [ ] The install-card on jsm.md is live and functional end-to-end — one of us has successfully installed the skill via the paste flow at least once on staging or prod.

## Acceptance criteria
- This bead stays open until the article ships with working (not placeholder) install URLs.
- Close with a comment noting which of the two framings was used at ship time.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:51:12.241720160Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T22:51:12.241720160Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.32","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:51:12.241720160Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.4","title":"Scaffold article component (components/wills-estate-article.tsx)","description":"Create `components/wills-estate-article.tsx` as a "use client" component modeled exactly on `components/slack-migration-article.tsx`.

## Scope of this bead (scaffold only — sections land in follow-up beads)
- Imports: React, Next `Image`, Next fonts (Crimson_Pro, JetBrains_Mono, Bricolage_Grotesque), lucide-react icons (tentative: FileDown, ScrollText, ShieldCheck, HeartPulse, HandCoins, Landmark, Sparkles, BookOpen), `MathTooltip`, `getJargon` from `@/lib/wills-estate-jargon`, `getScrollMetrics` from `@/lib/utils`, and illustration asset.
- Dynamic imports for the five visualizations (TierTriageViz, AxiomCoherenceViz, IntakePhasesViz, DeliverablesTreeViz, AntiPatternCardsViz) from `./wills-estate-visualizations`, each `{ ssr: false }` because they use browser APIs / framer-motion.
- Font variables set up the same way as slack article (`--font-crimson`, etc).
- Wrapper components defined locally (these can be copy-pasted from slack article with the `sm-` → `wep-` class-name rename if any styles diverge; otherwise keep `sm-` so we share the stylesheet): `J` (jargon tooltip), `EC` (editorial container, 800px centered), `Divider`, `Code`, `Mono`, `SectionHeader`, `Sub`, `RefTable`.
- Primer download button constant: `PRIMER_HREF = "/wills-and-estate-planning-primer.md"`, `PRIMER_FILENAME = "WILLS_AND_ESTATE_PLANNING_PRIMER.md"`.
- Main exported component `WillsEstateArticle` with:
  - `scrollProgress` state + scroll listener (copy pattern from slack).
  - IntersectionObserver-driven section reveal (copy pattern from slack, reuses `sm-fade-section` / `sm-visible` classes in globals.css).
  - Outer `div` with ref, role="main", className combines `sm-scope sm-body` + font variable classes, same background `#020204` and text `#f8fafc`.
  - Scroll progress bar rendered at top.
  - Empty section placeholders (stubs with TODO comments) for each section bead to fill in: hero, download-cta, intro, catalog, install, who-this-is-for, core-insight, sample-session, produces, workflow, anti-patterns, verification, tldr.

## Why a single scaffold bead
Every content-section bead will modify this file. Scaffolding it once means downstream section beads can land in parallel without file-creation conflicts. Per AGENTS.md "Backwards Compatibility & File Sprawl" rule: one article component, not ten.

## Acceptance criteria
- File exists, `bun tsc --noEmit` is clean once the route + jargon beads land too.
- Has placeholder stubs for every section.
- Uses the existing `sm-*` CSS classes until we confirm we need a `wep-*` namespace (decided in the styles bead).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:35:20.549272285Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T22:43:55.142615976Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.4","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:35:20.549272285Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.4","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:43:55.142041930Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.5","title":"Register article in lib/content.ts + command palette","description":"Two small registrations so the article surfaces in the sites navigation:

## 1. `lib/content.ts`
Add an entry to whichever array drives the writing listing (likely `writingPosts` / `writing` / similar; grep for where slack-mattermost-migration is registered). Include:
- slug: `wills-and-estate-planning`
- title: final article title
- description: ~140 char hook suitable for the writing index card
- date: publication date
- tags: `["ai", "skills", "estate-planning", "wills", "life-planning"]`
- href: `/writing/wills-and-estate-planning`
- hero image reference

## 2. `components/command-palette.tsx` (Cmd+K search)
Add to the navigation/search array so `Cmd+K` → "wills" finds the article. Pattern: same as how the slack-mattermost article is registered.

## Why this bead is separate
These are tiny mechanical edits in existing registry files. Splitting from the article component bead avoids merge conflict with content sections and keeps the article discoverable the moment it goes live.

## Acceptance criteria
- Article shows up on `/writing`.
- `Cmd+K` → "wills" / "estate" / "planning" surfaces the article.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:35:31.551381415Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:15.911166538Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.5","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:35:31.551381415Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.5","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:15.910594517Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.6","title":"Write agent-readable primer markdown (public/wills-and-estate-planning-primer.md)","description":"Write `public/wills-and-estate-planning-primer.md` — a single-file primer that a paying subscriber can download from the article and paste into any fresh Claude / Codex / ChatGPT session to boot the agent into the wills-estate-planning mindset.

## Purpose
This is the offline-friendly equivalent of the skill itself. The full skill is 201 files, ~24k lines, ~1 MB — too big to paste into a context window. The primer is a lossy but coherent summary the agent can use even without the full skill installed. Acts as:

1. **Proof-of-value tease** for visitors who are not yet paying subscribers (they can see the shape of the skill).
2. **Emergency fallback** for a paying subscriber whose agent setup isnt cooperating — they can paste this and still get useful output.
3. **The thing the "Download the operator primer as Markdown" button on the article points at** (see `PRIMER_HREF` in the article scaffold bead).

## Structure (draws from SKILL.md at ~/jeffreys_skills/wills-and-estate-planning-skill/)
- `# Wills & Estate Planning — Operator Primer`
- TL;DR: what estate planning really is (coordination, not drafting), the 11 axioms condensed to the 5 most important ones, and the 2026 OBBBA $15M exemption reality.
- The kernel: axioms 0-11 in one-liner form with a sentence of why each matters.
- Wealth-tier routing (Tier 1-5 with one sentence each).
- The 9 intake phases as a numbered list.
- The 9-step workflow as a numbered list.
- The top 15 anti-patterns.
- The verification-first discipline (live-law checks).
- Output contract summary (intake/, analyses/, deliverables/).
- A short disclaimer: this primer is educational; an attorney signs the documents.
- Footer: "To get the full skill with all 17 subagents, 45 deliverable templates, and 135 reference files, subscribe at https://jeffreys-skills.md/ and install via the instructions at https://jeffreys-skills.md/skills/wills-and-estate-planning-skill"

## Format
- Plain Markdown.
- ~8-12 KB (smaller than slack primers 185 KB — this is deliberately a condensed version, not a complete operator guide).
- Semantic headings so an agent can index it.

## Acceptance criteria
- File exists at `public/wills-and-estate-planning-primer.md`.
- Articles download button successfully serves it as `application/octet-stream` with the configured filename.
- Content is self-contained: an agent with just this primer and no skill installed can still conduct a useful basic estate-planning intake.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:35:51.386730372Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T22:35:51.386730372Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.6","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:35:51.386730372Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
